title: Timeout Layers, Deadlines, Cancellation, Cleanup, and Budget Allocation · Browser and Node Client… · Contract Lens: Timeout Classification
summary: A search-as-you-type interface cancels stale requests with composed AbortSignals while preserving the winning result and announced status. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts timeout classification increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch end-to-end request budget and cancellation model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Report which phase and policy ended an operation without exposing secrets or claiming the remote effect is known. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Stop body processing, producers, timers, and queued work after cancellation and release every owned resource. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Fetch and TypeScript Predict Timeout Classification · Theory Timeout Classification
steps.instruction:
theory case — TypeScript Fetch evidence model has 600 ms end-to-end for DNS, connect, TLS, headers, streamed body, parse, and cleanup. A caller cancels during body read; allocate phase budgets, propagate the reason, stop owned work, and preserve unknown remote effect.
